title: EU Complex Product Appropriateness Test
regulatory_ref: MiFID II Article 25(3), PRIIPs KID Regulation
content:
This appropriateness assessment framework governs Meridian Wealth Partners' evaluation of client suitability for structured products classified as complex financial instruments under MiFID II Article 25(3). Given the aggressive risk profile inherent in these instruments, advisors must conduct enhanced due diligence before recommending products including but not limited to autocallable notes, reverse convertible bonds, barrier options, and multi-underlying structured certificates.

Client Knowledge Assessment Requirements: Advisors must verify that prospective investors demonstrate comprehensive understanding of derivative mechanics, correlation risks, and barrier breach scenarios. Minimum threshold requires clients to correctly answer 8 out of 10 technical questions covering volatility surfaces, knock-in provisions, and autocall mechanisms. Clients must articulate understanding of potential capital losses exceeding 75% in adverse market conditions, particularly for products with American barrier features or memory coupons.

Experience Verification Protocol: Acceptable client profiles include individuals with minimum three years active trading experience in options, futures, or structured products, with documented transaction history exceeding €250,000 annually. Alternative qualification includes professional credentials (CFA, FRM, CAIA) or institutional investment management experience of minimum five years. Clients lacking these qualifications require completion of our 40-hour structured products education program with supervised simulated trading.

Financial Situation Analysis: Structured product allocation must not exceed 15% of client's liquid net worth, with absolute minimum liquid assets of €500,000 exclusive of primary residence. For products with tenor exceeding three years, maximum allocation reduces to 10% given liquidity constraints and mark-to-market volatility. Clients approaching retirement (within seven years) face additional restrictions limiting exposure to 5% regardless of net worth, considering reduced risk tolerance and shortened investment horizon.

Product-Specific Risk Disclosure: In compliance with PRIIPs KID Regulation, advisors must review standardized Key Information Documents with clients, emphasizing performance scenarios under stressed market conditions. Particular attention must focus on Summary Risk Indicator (SRI) ratings of 6 or 7, explaining how combination risks exceed individual underlying asset volatility. For multi-asset structured products, correlation breakdown scenarios must be explicitly discussed, including historical examples from 2008 financial crisis and March 2020 COVID-related market disruption.

Documentation and Ongoing Monitoring: All appropriateness assessments require written client acknowledgment of understanding, retained for minimum seven years per regulatory requirements. Quarterly position monitoring mandatory for all structured product holdings, with immediate client contact required when unrealized losses exceed 25% or when underlying assets approach barrier levels. Annual suitability reviews must reassess client circumstances, particularly income stability, liquidity needs, and risk tolerance evolution.

Escalation Protocols: Any structured product recommendation exceeding client's stated risk parameters requires senior advisor approval and additional cooling-off period of 48 hours. Products with embedded leverage exceeding 200% or those referencing exotic underlyings (cryptocurrency, commodity volatility indices) mandate private wealth team consultation regardless of client net worth. This framework ensures compliance with enhanced investor protection standards while maintaining appropriate access to sophisticated investment strategies for qualified clients seeking enhanced return potential through structured product exposure.
